title: Travel agent — bubble render to execution-level + canvas wiring (AMBER round 5 → GREEN)
description: Round 5 closed AMBER with backend GREEN: all three intents complete, render payloads exist in step events, ops PR #52 landed the status→outcome rename. Two visibility blockers remain: (1) execution.result.render is empty because the playbook's tail step `end` was a noop — the auto-render watcher and the report command both rely on top-level execution.result.render, and the watcher's event walk apparently doesn't surface it cleanly either; (2) the /travel canvas was still wired to the legacy api_integration/amadeus_ai_api playbook because gatewayAuth.ts hardcoded that path in `executeGatewayPlaybook`'s GraphQL call, separately from the GatewayAssistant.tsx constant I'd already updated. Claude has shipped both fixes in working tree, NOT committed: (a) replaced the `end` noop with a python step that pulls the selected render_* step's payload into the execution-level result (so execution.result.render is populated for any branch — flights, locations, help, amadeus_failure); (b) updated gatewayAuth.ts PLAYBOOK_NAME to automation/agents/travel/runtime; (c) updated the canvas helper text to match. Cap: 1 ops PR + 1 gui PR.

files_claude_wrote.repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runti: modified — `end` step replaced from `kind: noop` to `kind: python`. The new tail reads each render_* step's result via `{{ render_X | default({}) }}` inputs and selects whichever branch fired (only one runs per execution because the log_classification arcs are exclusive). It mirrors the chosen step's `render` widget tree into execution.result.render, plus `text`/`summary`/`intent`/`outcome`/`upstream_status_code` for the audit trail. The GUI watcher's extractAgentRender finds the widget at the top of execution.result without needing to walk events.
files_claude_wrote.repos/gui/src/services/gatewayAuth.ts: modified — PLAYBOOK_NAME constant updated from 'api_integration/amadeus_ai_api' to 'automation/agents/travel/runtime'. The legacy fixture stays in repos/e2e for smoke purposes; the canvas's gateway-mode path now targets the flagship agent runtime. Added a comment explaining the rename.
files_claude_wrote.repos/gui/src/components/GatewayAssistan: modified — the canvas's helper text in the header was still describing 'api_integration/amadeus_ai_api'. Updated to describe the new flagship agent runtime and note that results render as widgets below the chat bubble.

context_from_amber_round_5.what_passed: All three terminal intents reach status=completed (help in 4.8s, flights in 9.0s, locations in 9.0s). render_tree_present=true on every execution. Backend convention fix proven (status:failed → outcome:amadeus_failure).
context_from_amber_round_5.what_failed: Both visibility assertions: prompt history shows result=- (execution.result.render empty) and the /travel canvas displayed the legacy playbook's wording + 422 on POST /execute.
context_from_amber_round_5.diagnosis: The travel runtime's tail was a noop, so execution.result was empty. The GUI watcher's extractAgentRender DOES walk events as a fallback, but Codex's smoke shows it didn't surface the widget — likely because event payloads are paginated and the relevant event isn't in the first page, OR the projection lossy-strips render in event records. Top-level execution.result.render is the reliable carrier. Separately, gatewayAuth.ts had its own PLAYBOOK_NAME constant that I missed when wiring GatewayAssistant.tsx in the flagship round.

spec: `noetl validate repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml`
`cd repos/gui && npm install && npx tsc --noEmit` — Claude verified locally; expect exit=0.
`cd repos/gui && npm run build`.

3_open_and_merge_ops_pr.objective: Branch, commit, push, PR, merge the playbook tail-bubble fix.
spec: `cd repos/ops && git checkout -b kadyapam/travel-bubble-render-to-execution`.
Stage automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml.
Commit using `git commit -F -` heredoc. Conventional-commits: `fix(travel-agent): bubble selected render branch into execution-level result so GUI watcher attaches widget`.
Push, PR, merge with `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch`.
3_open_and_merge_ops_pr.verify: PR merged. Capture PR URL + merge SHA.

4_open_and_merge_gui_pr.objective: Branch, commit, push, PR, merge the gateway/canvas wiring update.
spec: `cd repos/gui && git checkout -b kadyapam/canvas-target-travel-agent`.
Stage src/services/gatewayAuth.ts + src/components/GatewayAssistant.tsx.

Commit: `fix(gui): canvas + gateway path target the new travel agent playbook`. Body explains: gatewayAuth.ts had its own PLAYBOOK_NAME hardcode that was missed in the flagship round; helper text in the canvas header also referenced the legacy playbook. Both updated.
PR title: same.
Push, PR, merge. semantic-release will likely cut v1.10.x or v1.11.0.
4_open_and_merge_gui_pr.verify: PR merged. Capture PR URL + merge SHA + new GUI release tag.

5_redeploy_and_register.objective: Deploy patched GUI + re-register the patched travel runtime.
spec: Wait for GUI release tag.
`noetl exec noetl/lifecycle/bump_image --runtime local --payload '{"deployment":"gui","namespace":"noetl","image":"ghcr.io/noetl/gui:<TAG>"}'`.
kubectl rollout status deploy/gui --timeout=180s.
Pull merged ops at HEAD; `noetl register repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml` (now version 6).
Capture new catalog_id + version.

6_smoke_terminal_widget_visibility.objective: Replay all three intents — widgets MUST appear inline in the prompt history.
spec: `travel help` — expected: status=completed AND a fresh prompt entry with the help widget appears below the started entry within ~10 seconds (auto-render watcher).
`travel flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15 for 2 adults` — same: widget visible inline, either flight carousel (Amadeus 200) or friendly error (Amadeus 5xx).
`travel locations near Boston` — same.
Run `report <execution_id>` for one of them — expected: report shows the textual summary AND the widget below it (no more `result=-`).
Capture screenshots showing widgets actually inline in the prompt.
6_smoke_terminal_widget_visibility.verify: Widgets visible in prompt history for all three intents. Report command surfaces widgets.

7_smoke_canvas_widget_visibility.objective: Travel canvas surfaces widgets and uses the new playbook.
spec: Navigate to /travel. The header copy should now mention the flagship agent runtime, not the legacy playbook.
Type a flight query. Expected: chat bubble + widget below — no more 422 on POST /execute.
Click rerun in the widget; canvas re-runs.
Capture screenshot.
7_smoke_canvas_widget_visibility.verify: Canvas works against the new playbook with widget rendering.

8_smoke_amadeus_mcp_regression.objective: Confirm MCP playbook still works after the playbook tail change (regression check).
spec: `cd /mcp/amadeus`; `tools` (5); `call get_token` (token_bound=true); `call search_locations keyword=Boston`; `call search_flights origin=SFO destination=JFK departureDate=2026-07-15`.
All should complete at the noetl level (urllib wrappers in place; envelope shape preserved).
`cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta && git submodule update --remote repos/ops repos/gui`.
`git add repos/ops repos/gui`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump ops + gui for travel render bubble + canvas wiring (flagship GREEN)`.
Don't push ai-meta.
9_bump_ai_meta_pointers.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit on top of existing 26 unpushed commits.

expected_state.description: Tail step bubbles render to execution-level result. Canvas + gateway target the new playbook. All three terminal intents show widgets inline. Canvas shows widgets. ai-meta gitlinks staged.
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; TAIL_FIX=$(grep -c 'Bubble the selected render branch' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); GUI_FIX=$(grep -c 'automation/agents/travel/runtime' repos/gui/src/services/gatewayAuth.ts 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "tail_fix:$TAIL_FIX gui_fix:$GUI_FIX"; if [ "$TAIL_FIX" -ge 1 ] && [ "$GUI_FIX" -ge 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: TRAVEL_BUBBLE_RENDER_AND_CANVAS_LANDED'; else echo 'GOAL: NOT_YET'; fi

denied_tools: Tweaking Claude's playbook or TypeScript code beyond obvious typos
preferred_tools: cd repos/gui && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build
git apply / git commit -F - / git push (2 topic branches: ops + gui)
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch / gh release view
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260509-063112-travel-bubble-render-and-canvas-amber-to-green.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_validate, 2_typecheck_and_build_gui, 3_open_and_merge_ops_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha}, 4_open_and_merge_gui_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha, gui_release_tag}, 5_redeploy_and_register: {gui_tag, travel_catalog_id, travel_version}, 6_smoke_terminal_widget_visibility: {help_widget_visible, flights_widget_visible, locations_widget_visible, report_command_shows_widget, screenshots: []}, 7_smoke_canvas_widget_visibility: {execution_id, widget_visible, header_copy_updated, screenshot}, 8_smoke_amadeus_mcp_regression: {get_token_bound, search_flights_status, search_locations_status}, 9_bump_ai_meta_pointers: {ops_sha, gui_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups}. If verdict GREEN, append a paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md noting the travel agent flagship round closed GREEN.
